title: The WhatsApp Group Where The Game Never Happens
hook: 40+ unread messages. Still no venue. Still no game.

body.caption:
You know the one — the WhatsApp group where 'let's play Saturday' turns into forty-plus unread messages, three dead polls, and still no venue picked. Two weeks later, nobody's played anything.

On KeepItUp, the game itself is the plan. A host sets a real date, time, and number of spots. You ask to join, they say yes, and the group chat only opens once people are actually coming — not before anyone's confirmed.

No more 'who's in?' No more waiting on ten replies to a poll that dies anyway. Just a game, a place, a time.

KeepItUp is open worldwide — early access, link in bio.
body.cta: Open worldwide — early access, link in bio.

Base photo: adults playing flag football at golden hour — the real game actually happening, as the payoff/contrast. (Composite baked: b8p1-groupchat-meme.png)

title: You Paid €40 To Stand At A Bar With A Name Tag
hook: Ever paid for one of those singles nights? Name tag, drink, forty minutes of small talk on a timer.

body.caption:
Ever paid for one of those singles nights? You get a drink, a name tag, and forty minutes of trying to think of something to say to a stranger before a timer moves you along to the next one.

KeepItUp skips all of that. You show up to a real game — football, running, whatever you're into — and you meet people by actually doing something with them. No ticket, no name tag, no small talk on a clock.

It's free. Sometimes it turns into a date, sometimes a friendship, sometimes just a great Tuesday.

KeepItUp is open worldwide — early access, link in bio.
body.cta: Open worldwide — early access, link in bio.
body.imageConcept: Clean photo, caption-led, no overlay — native/authentic treatment, cheeky but kind tone. Base photo: two adult men passing a football in a park, casual and unforced.

title: The Honest Cost Comparison
hook: A typical singles-event ticket: around €35–45. A game on KeepItUp: free.

body.caption:
Let's do the actual math. A typical singles mixer or speed-dating night costs somewhere around €35 to €45 a ticket — sometimes more if a drink's included. You go, you stand around, maybe you meet someone, maybe you don't.

A game on KeepItUp costs nothing. You find one near you, ask to join, and go play. If you meet someone, great. If you just get a good run in with new people, also great.

We're not against paying for things. We just don't think meeting people should have to be one of them.

KeepItUp is open worldwide — early access, link in bio.
body.cta: Open worldwide — early access, link in bio.

title: Organizing A Game: There Vs. Here
hook: This is what organizing a game usually looks like. This is what it looks like on KeepItUp.

slides: THERE: 40+ messages. Three polls. Still deciding on a venue.
HERE: One listing. A time, a place, a yes. Show up and play.
body.caption:
This is what organizing a game usually looks like: a long group chat, a poll nobody finishes, and a venue that never gets picked.

This is what it looks like on KeepItUp: someone posts a real game — sport, time, place, how many spots. You ask to join. They say yes. You show up.

Same friends, same sport, way less chat.

KeepItUp is open worldwide — early access, link in bio.
body.cta: Open worldwide — early access, link in bio.

(Both slides baked: b8p4-there/here.png)

title: Why Doing Something Together Beats Talking About It
hook: A 2000 study found pairs who did something new together, not just talked, felt closer afterward.

body.caption:
There's a well-known study from 2000 by researcher Arthur Aron and colleagues. They had couples do something new and a bit exciting together, or something routine, and measured how close they felt afterward. The pairs who did something new together — not just talked — felt closer.

We think the same logic holds before people even know each other well. A profile and a few messages only tell you so much. Ten minutes on a football pitch or a group run tells you how someone actually shows up — do they laugh things off, do they check if you're okay, do they actually enjoy themselves.

Talking is a fine start. Doing something together is what tells you if you want to keep talking.

KeepItUp is open worldwide — early access, link in bio.
body.cta: Open worldwide — early access, link in bio.
body.imageConcept: Blend treatment: small label top-left on a small dark scrim ('WHY DOING BEATS TALKING'), photo otherwise clean (overlay to be baked); full explanation lives in the caption. Base photo: people jogging together on a bridge, contemplative mood. Source: Aron, Norman, Aron, McKenna & Heyman (2000), Journal of Personality and Social Psychology, 78(2), 273-284 — shared novel/exciting activity (not just talk) measurably increased felt closeness in lab experiments with couples; we generalize the mechanism honestly, without claiming it was tested on strangers. (Composite baked: b8p5-doing-beats-talking.png)

title: It's Free. Here's What You Get.
hook: No ticket, no subscription. Here's exactly what KeepItUp gives you, for free.

body.caption:
Quick and simple: KeepItUp is free. No ticket, no subscription to meet people.

Here's what you get: small local games and runs near you, in whatever sport you like. You ask to join one, the host says yes or no, and if it's a yes, you get the exact time, place, and a group chat with the people actually coming.

You can be there for dating, for friends, or just for a group to play with — all three are welcome, and you say which one when you sign up.

That's it. Real people, a real game, no cost.

KeepItUp is open worldwide — early access, link in bio.
